Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The European AllSky7 fireball network 90 upvotes, 7 comments
  2. Whistler: Live eBPF Programming from the Common Lisp REPL 115 upvotes, 14 comments
  3. OpenTelemetry profiles enters public alpha 183 upvotes, 28 comments
  4. Why so many control rooms were seafoam green (2025) 911 upvotes, 190 comments
  5. Chroma Context-1: Training a Self-Editing Search Agent 59 upvotes, 8 comments
  6. Generators in Lone Lisp 53 upvotes, 8 comments
  7. DOOM Over DNS 322 upvotes, 85 comments
  8. Local Bernstein theory, and lower bounds for Lebesgue constants 27 upvotes, 3 comments
  9. Show HN: I put an AI agent on a $7/month VPS with IRC as its transport layer 287 upvotes, 81 comments
  10. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 914 upvotes, 319 comments
  11. QRV Operating System: QNX on RISC-V 37 upvotes, 7 comments
  12. My minute-by-minute response to the LiteLLM malware attack 399 upvotes, 150 comments
  13. HyperAgents: Self-referential self-improving agents 215 upvotes, 76 comments
  14. An unstoppable mushroom is tearing through North American forests 104 upvotes, 42 comments
  15. Hong Kong Police Can Now Demand Phone Passwords Under New Security Rules 22 upvotes, 5 comments
  16. Agent-to-agent pair programming 99 upvotes, 40 comments
  17. Using FireWire on a Raspberry Pi 106 upvotes, 46 comments
  18. Anthropic Subprocessor Changes 99 upvotes, 44 comments
  19. $500 GPU outperforms Claude Sonnet on coding benchmarks 386 upvotes, 218 comments
  20. The 'Paperwork Flood': How I Drowned a Bureaucrat Before Dinner 172 upvotes, 90 comments
  21. A Faster Alternative to Jq 233 upvotes, 134 comments
  22. Gzip decompression in 250 lines of Rust 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  23. We haven't seen the worst of what gambling and prediction markets will do 821 upvotes, 573 comments
  24. This picture broke my brain [3B1B video] 39 upvotes, 21 comments
  25. Everything old is new again: memory optimization 100 upvotes, 67 comments
  26. Apple discontinues the Mac Pro 504 upvotes, 412 comments
  27. Schedule tasks on the web 212 upvotes, 168 comments
  28. Hold on to Your Hardware 319 upvotes, 268 comments
  29. The Legibility of Serif and Sans Serif Typefaces (2022) 61 upvotes, 41 comments
  30. We rewrote JSONata with AI in a day, saved $500k/year 214 upvotes, 192 comments

Last generated 10:30pm, 27 March 2026. Source here.
